+def build_classifier_from_state(state):

+    """Detect classifier architecture from state dict keys."""

+    # Find all keys under 'classifier.' (after stripping 'module.' prefix)

+    classifier_keys = {}

+    for k, v in state.items():

+        new_k = k

+        if new_k.startswith('module.'):

+            new_k = new_k[7:]

+        if new_k.startswith('classifier.'):

+            subkey = new_k[len('classifier.'):]

+            classifier_keys[subkey] = v

+    if not classifier_keys:

+        # Assume linear classifier with default hidden_dim (will be set later)

+        return None, None

+    # Presume linear if only 'weight' and 'bias' exist

+    if 'weight' in classifier_keys and 'bias' in classifier_keys and len(classifier_keys) == 2:

+        return nn.Linear(classifier_keys['weight'].shape[1], classifier_keys['weight'].shape[0]), 'linear'

+    # Else assume sequential MLP

+    # Parse numeric indices

+    layer_indices = set()

+    for key in classifier_keys:

+        parts = key.split('.')

+        if parts[0].isdigit():

+            layer_indices.add(int(parts[0]))

+    if not layer_indices:

+        # Fallback to one linear layer

+        if 'weight' in classifier_keys:

+            return nn.Linear(classifier_keys['weight'].shape[1], classifier_keys['weight'].shape[0]), 'linear'

+        return None, None

+    # Build sequential

+    layers = []

+    max_idx = max(layer_indices)

+    for i in range(max_idx + 1):

+        weight_key = f'{i}.weight'

+        bias_key = f'{i}.bias'

+        if weight_key not in classifier_keys:

+            continue

+        weight = classifier_keys[weight_key]

+        bias = classifier_keys.get(bias_key, None)

+        out_features, in_features = weight.shape[0], weight.shape[1]

+        layers.append((f'{i}', nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)))

+        # Add ReLU after non-final layers

+        if i < max_idx:

+            # Check if next layer exists

+            layers.append((f'relu_{i}', nn.ReLU(inplace=True)))

+            # Optionally add dropout if exists; we don't have dropout keys so skip

+    return nn.Sequential(OrderedDict(layers)), 'mlp'

+

+

+class AdaptableClassifier(nn.Module):

+    """Wrapper that combines CLIP vision encoder with an auto-detected classifier."""

+    def __init__(self, model_name, num_classes, checkpoint_state):

+        super().__init__()

+        self.clip = CLIPModel.from_pretrained(model_name)

+        # Freeze text encoder (not used)

+        for param in self.clip.text_model.parameters():

+            param.requires_grad = False

+        # Determine hidden_dim

+        with torch.no_grad():

+            dummy = torch.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)

+            out = self.clip.vision_model(pixel_values=dummy)

+            hidden_dim = out.pooler_output.shape[-1]

+        classifier, ctype = build_classifier_from_state(checkpoint_state)

+        if classifier is None:

+            self.classifier = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, num_classes)

+        else:

+            # If linear and dimensions match, use it; else create new

+            if ctype == 'linear' and classifier.in_features == hidden_dim and classifier.out_features == num_classes:

+                self.classifier = classifier

+            else:

+                # In case dimensions mismatch (shouldn't happen), create a new linear with correct dims

+                self.classifier = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, num_classes)

+        # Freeze irrelevant parts (optional)

+        if hasattr(self.clip, 'logit_scale'):

+            self.clip.logit_scale.requires_grad = False

+        if hasattr(self.clip, 'text_projection'):

+            self.clip.text_projection.requires_grad = False

+

+    def forward(self, pixel_values):

+        vision_outputs = self.clip.vision_model(pixel_values=pixel_values)

+        pooled = vision_outputs.pooler_output

+        logits = self.classifier(pooled)

+        return logits

+

+
